parsed_review.summary: CP00-753 is a clean descriptor-only continuation of the Korean Legal Depth (RP24) bridge-pack pattern (CP722-CP752), covering RP24.P09.M04.S05 through RP24.P09.M06.S02 (40 units, risk_class B). Independent read-only review (Read/Grep/Glob only) confirms the pack does not overclaim: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, writes_product_state=false, runtime_opened=false, and pack_level_claude_review status=pending (valid_review_receipts=[]). Deliverable distribution {test:2, ui:2, implementation:27, claude_review:6, security_audit:2, hermes_evidence:1} sums to 40 and matches across the plan baseline, registry KOREAN_LEGAL_CP753_REQUIREMENTS, manifest deliverables, and contract validation.packs.cp753 (coverage.valid=true, errors=[], by_deliverable exact). Section-row slices yield M04=16 / M05=22 / M06=2 with unit titles matching the plan. Authority/safety boundaries are enforced in code and attestations: no-write, no-real-data (grep found only guard strings such as real_client_data_included:false), claude read_only=true and claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true with runtime_opening_pack_id=CP00-754. Hermes control-plane patterns are deterministic review-packet/receipt only (createHermesEvidencePacket emits_runtime_receipt=false; hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false) and are not wired into product runtime. CP178+ review-hardening checks hold: auth preflight passed (exit 0, stdout 1445 bytes, valid JSON result {"ok":true}), no zero-byte stdout, no fenced-JSON-with-prose, no tool-call-shaped output, no missing required fields; the forbidden_review_evidence set is encoded in registry requirements and tools are restricted to Read/Grep/Glob under read_only/dontAsk. The recorded validation ladder in command-evidence.json (node --check x6, rp24:validate, rp24:korean-legal-depth:validate, node --test, npm test/validate/spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane, git diff --check, build, review baseline/ready) all report exit_code 0. One non-blocking P3 records the expected reviewer-provenance boundary: as a read-only reviewer I confirmed the ladder via recorded receipts plus direct source inspection, not independent re-execution. No P0/P1/P2 findings. This verdict closes only the CP00-753 closeout goal; final human/enterprise-trust approval and any runtime opening remain out of scope and deferred to CP00-754.

findings.title: Validation ladder verified from recorded receipts and direct source inspection, not independent re-execution
findings.details: As the read-only reviewer restricted to Read/Grep/Glob (no Bash), the CP00-753 validation ladder (node --check across korean-legal sources and validators, rp24:validate, rp24:korean-legal-depth:validate, node --test packages/korean-legal/test/model.test.js, npm test/validate/spec:requirements/weighted/fullplan/goal:closeout/rp00:control-plane, git diff --check, npm run build, and the review baseline/ready steps) was confirmed via the recorded command-evidence.json receipts (all exit_code 0), the readiness-report.json auth_preflight (passed, valid JSON result), and direct inspection of registry.js, service.js, validators.js, test/model.test.js, the contract, and the contract validator script — rather than by independently re-running the commands. This is the inherent and expected read-only reviewer boundary, not a defect, and matches the provenance note recorded for the prior pack CP00-752.
findings.recommendation: No code or pack action required. Continue to treat the deterministic CI/Hermes control-plane gates as the authoritative re-execution authority, and keep validation outcomes provenance-tagged as [recorded receipts + source inspection] rather than [independent re-execution] when surfaced in adjudication.
findings.post_review_disposition: Acknowledged as the expected read-only reviewer provenance boundary; recorded as an informational P3 that does not block CP00-753 pack closeout or the CP00-753 closeout goal. Final human/enterprise-trust approval and any runtime opening remain deferred to CP00-754.
